Workplace accidents include all accidents causing harm to human health or death and that are related to work at the workplace, while working off-site or during home office/remote work and all accidents on the direct way between home and workplace on the way to and from work.
DGUV - Overview of Occupational Accident Insurance in Germany BMAS - What Qualifies as an Occupational Accident in Germany SGB VII §8 - Legal Definition of Occupational Accidents (Arbeitsunfälle)

Include hours worked by salaried, hourly, part-time and contract workers including temporary help services workers. Do not include vacation, sick leave, holidays, or any other non-work time, even if employees were paid for it. If your establishment keeps records of only the hours paid or if you have employees who are not paid by the hour, please estimate the hours that the employees actually worked. If this number isn't available, an estimate is still needed. In Germany, maximum is 250 working days / year (rest is weekends and banking holidays), minus 28 days vacation (average), thus (250-28) x 8 = ± 1.800 "productive" working hours / year More information on Annual Working Hours can be found on the Bundesverwaltungsamt website.
Please enter the total number of hours worked by all employees during the given year. Please round your answer to the next highest whole number.
ArbSchG § 6 (2)\n
Please enter the number of work related fatalities for the given year.
ArbSchG § 6 (2) of the German Occupational Safety and Health Act provides more information on work-related fatalities. Terms and definitions can be found directly on the DGUV website.

Please enter the number of accidents that are classified as 4 days or more of absence (excluding fatalities) i.e. an injury which resulted in the employee being unable to carry out their normal work duties for more than three consecutive days, excluding the day of the accident for the given year.
An injury which resulted in the employee being unable to carry out their normal work duties for three or less consecutive days, excluding the day of the accident for the given year.
Please enter the number of accidents that are classified as three day injuries or less (excluding fatalities).
First Aid accidents refer to work-related incidents that required only basic, on-site first aid treatment and did not result in lost work time beyond the day of the incident.
Please enter the number of First Aid accidents.

Report the number of legally reportable dangerous occurrences (“meldepflichtige gefährliche Ereignisse”), as defined by German law. Only include serious incidents or near misses specified in the official guidance. Leitfaden meldepflichtige Ereignisse im Sinne der Störfall-Verordnung

### High Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *HIGHER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.

### Low Work Hours:

Are your hours and/or employee count entered correctly? The total number of hours reported are considerably *LOWER* than the average annual hours typically worked by full-time and part-time employees.

Please validate that you have entered your correct employee count and/or total hours. If correct, provide a brief explanation for approval (this will be visible to your client connections). Otherwise, please correct your answers.
